quests_completed: SQL Raid: Restructure Indian Govt CPI Data

daily_quests.title: ASX 200 Price Momentum Analysis with Window Functions
daily_quests.description: Using ASX 200 historical price data, write a query that calculates the 20-day moving average price for each stock, ranks stocks by their current price relative to their 20-day average (highest performers first), and identifies stocks where price crossed above their moving average in the last 5 trading days. Use window functions (AVG() OVER, ROW_NUMBER(), LAG()) and CTEs to structure your solution. Expected output: stock ticker, current price, 20-day moving average, momentum rank, and crossover flag (Y/N).

daily_quests.title: NSW Road Crash Data Cleaning & Aggregation Pipeline
daily_quests.description: Download NSW Road Crash Data (contains messy, incomplete records with inconsistent date formats, missing postcodes, and duplicate entries). Write a Python script using pandas that: (1) standardises date columns to YYYY-MM-DD format, (2) removes duplicate crash records based on crash ID and timestamp, (3) handles missing postcodes by forward-filling from the same local government area, (4) creates a severity category column (Minor/Moderate/Severe/Fatal based on injury count), (5) exports a cleaned CSV with row counts before/after cleaning logged to console.

daily_quests.title: Australian Weather Anomaly Detection & Storage Pipeline
daily_quests.description: Create an end-to-end pipeline: (1) Use Python + pandas to load Australian Bureau of Meteorology daily weather observations for the past 90 days, (2) calculate monthly temperature anomalies (deviation from 30-year historical mean — assume you have a reference CSV with mean temps by month/location), (3) identify days where temperature exceeded mean + 2 standard deviations, (4) create a cleaned dataset with anomaly flags and confidence scores, (5) write a SQL query to load this data into a staging table and generate a summary report showing: location, anomaly count, max temperature spike, and affected date range. Output: (a) cleaned CSV, (b) SQL INSERT statements, (c) anomaly summary report.
daily_quests.dataset: Australian Weather Observations — Bureau of Meteorology (Kaggle: jsphyg)
